urgency.message: 4 new CISA KEV addition(s) this week. Patch validation recommended.

items.title: New Prinz Eugen ransomware prioritizes recent files for encryption
items.summary: A new ransomware operation named 'Prinz Eugen' prioritizes recently modified files for encryption and leaves no ransom note on the system. [...]

items.title: Microsoft links Mastra AI supply chain attack to North Korean hackers
items.summary: Microsoft has attributed a recent Mastra AI supply chain attack that compromised more than 140 npm packages to the North Korean hacking group Sapphire Sleet, also known as BlueNoroff. [...]

items.title: Hackers Exploit Gravity SMTP WordPress Plugin Bug to Expose API Keys
items.summary:
Threat actors are exploiting a recently patched security flaw impacting Gravity SMTP, a WordPress plugin that's installed on about 100,000 sites.

The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-4020 (CVSS score: 5.3), is a medium-severity information disclosure flaw that can allow unauthenticated attackers

items.title: French President Urges US to Share Cutting-Edge AI and Democracies to Cooperate on Regulation
items.summary:
French President Emmanuel Macron urged the world’s wealthy democracies to work together on regulating advanced AI systems.
The post French President Urges US to Share Cutting-Edge AI and Democracies to Cooperate on Regulation appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Hackers Claim to Leak Stolen Madison Square Garden Data
items.summary: Plus: Gay bars in San Francisco using face scanners, France quits Palantir, Apple plans to change its private email and more.

items.title: Threat Brief: Mitigating Large-Scale Credential Attacks
items.summary:
We provide guidance for preparing for and mitigating large-scale credential attacks, focusing on recent campaigns targeting security vendors' devices.

items.title: Klue OAuth breach victim list grows as Icarus hackers claim attack
items.summary: Market intelligence platform Klue has publicly confirmed a recent security incident that allowed threat actors to steal OAuth tokens used to connect to customers' Salesforce environments, as the new "Icarus" extortion group publicly claims the attack. [...]

items.title: Hackers exploit info disclosure bug in Gravity SMTP WordPress plugin
items.summary: Threat actors are exploiting an unauthenticated information disclosure vulnerability in the WordPress plugin Gravity SMTP, active on 100,000 sites. [...]

items.title: Unpatchable 'usbliter8' Exploit Breaks Apple A12 and A13 SecureROM Boot Chain
items.summary:
Security researchers at Paradigm Shift have published a working exploit, dubbed usbliter8, that achieves arbitrary code execution inside the SecureROM of Apple's A12 and A13 chips.

That code is burned into the silicon at manufacture. No software update can reach it. Affected devices

items.title: The Gentlemen RaaS Uses GentleKiller EDR Framework Targeting 400 Security Processes
items.summary:
The Gentlemen ransomware-as-a-service (RaaS) operation is actively developing and maintaining a suite of endpoint detection and response (EDR) killers that it hands out to affiliates for impairing system defenses before deploying the encryptor.

This mature portfolio of EDR-terminating tools is cent

items.title: Texas govt data breach exposes over 3 million driver’s licenses
items.summary: The Texas Parks and Wildlife Department (TPWD) disclosed a data breach at its license system vendor that exposed personal information for more than three million individuals. [...]

items.title: AutoJack Attack Lets One Web Page Hijack AI Agent for Host Code Execution
items.summary:
Microsoft researchers have detailed an exploit chain, named AutoJack, that turns an AI browsing agent into a delivery vehicle for remote code execution.

Steer the agent to load an attacker's web page, and that page's JavaScript can reach a privileged local service on the same machine and spawn

items.summary:

items.title: Operation Endgame Disrupts SocGholish Servers, Cleans 14,971 WordPress Sites
items.summary:
Dutch law enforcement authorities, along with counterparts from  Canada  , Germany, and the U.S., have disrupted malicious infrastructure associated with  SocGholish  and cleaned up nearly 15,000 infected WordPress websites.

"With these actions we deprive cybercriminals of access to infected comput

items.title: CISA Warns Fortinet Customers as FortiBleed Hits 86,644 FortiGate Devices
items.summary:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Thursday urged Fortinet customers with FortiGate appliances to take steps to secure against ongoing malicious activity aimed at thousands of internet-accessible devices.

The sweeping campaign, believed to be the work of Russian-spe

items.title: Every AI Agent Is an Identity. Most Organizations Don't Treat Them That Way
items.summary: AI agents can access data, trigger workflows, deploy code, and interact with critical business systems, often with little oversight. Token Security breaks down why AI agents are becoming a new identity and governance challenge. [...]

items.title: The Good, the Bad and the Ugly in Cybersecurity – Week 25
items.summary: FBI dismantles extensive PhaaS, DragonForce ransomware abuses MS Teams relays, and PRC-based spies breach REDCap servers to steal research data.

items.title: Stressors, AI Forcing Changes to Cybersecurity Teams
items.summary: As threats proliferate and AI complicates cybersecurity, CISOs say the job is getting harder, but more companies still want cybersecurity expertise, if even on a part-time basis.

items.title: Webinar: How attackers bypass MFA and how defenders can respond
items.summary: Modern phishing attacks, including Device Code phishing, can undermine MFA protections and grant attackers access to corporate accounts without stealing passwords. This webinar explores how behavioral AI can help security teams detect compromised accounts faster and automate response workflows. [...

items.title: From Assistive to Agentic: The AI Shift That's Redefining Threat Management
items.summary:
Introduction

The average enterprise security team has 40 or more security tools, giving a lot of visibility into internal telemetry and asset data. But often, these tools are working in siloes, generating (overlapping) alerts and data. And yet, breach dwell times remain stubbornly long (~43 days),

items.title: Microsoft: June 2026 Windows updates break Recycle Bin prompts
items.summary: Microsoft has confirmed a confusing Windows bug that causes different filenames to appear in the confirmation dialog when deleting a file from the Recycle Bin. [...]

items.title: CryptoBandits Malware Doubles as a Backdoor, Abuses Tor
items.summary:
CryptoBandits uses a local SOCKS5 proxy for traffic routing, blending data theft with remote code execution.
The post CryptoBandits Malware Doubles as a Backdoor, Abuses Tor appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: AWS Unveils 'Continuum,' an AI-Powered Vulnerability Management Platform
items.summary: Working with frontier AI models, this new platform aims to help discovering, prioritizing, validating and remediating code vulnerabilities

items.title: FortiBleed: 86,000 Fortinet Device Credentials Compromised
items.summary:
The large-scale credential theft campaign hit roughly half of the internet-accessible Fortinet firewalls and VPNs.
The post FortiBleed: 86,000 Fortinet Device Credentials Compromised appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Forget Data Leakage: Shadow AI's Real Threat Is Access Control
items.summary:
The first wave of enterprise AI concern was straightforward. It was simply employees pasting sensitive data into public AI tools. Security teams responded with usage policies, domain blocks, and data loss prevention rules. That response made sense at the time.

It doesn't fit the problem anymore.

items.summary:
The hackers exfiltrated data from Salesforce instances of Klue customers, such as Huntress and Recorded Future.

items.title: Confidence Lacks in Threat Detection Across Non-Email Channels like Slack and Teams
items.summary: Half of cybersecurity leaders lack confidence in detecting threats on Slack, Teams and other non-email platforms, despite growing attacker focus

items.summary: I detected an interesting phishing email this morning. It targets a major Belgian bank:

items.title: Cisco to Acquire WideField Security to Boost Splunk’s Agentic SOC
items.summary:
WideField will accelerate Agentic SOC capabilities by expanding the lens on threat investigation to include identity, credentials, sessions, and blast radius.
The post Cisco to Acquire WideField Security to Boost Splunk’s Agentic SOC appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: 15,000 WordPress Websites Cleaned Up in SocGholish Botnet Takedown
items.summary:
Law enforcement and private partners took down 106 SocGholish C&C servers and domains as part of Operation Endgame.
The post 15,000 WordPress Websites Cleaned Up in SocGholish Botnet Takedown  appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Splunk Enterprise Vulnerability Exploited in Attacks Days After Disclosure
items.summary:
CISA has given federal agencies only three days to patch CVE-2026-20253, which can be exploited for unauthenticated remote code execution.
The post Splunk Enterprise Vulnerability Exploited in Attacks Days After Disclosure appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Azure AD Graph Activity Logs: Ingestion and threat detection to close the visibility gap
items.summary: Azure AD Graph Activity Logs land in Elastic with full ECS parsing. Detect ROADrecon and AADInternals enumeration with ready-to-use detection rules.

items.title: Lost in relocation: analysis of a new loader distributing CASTLESTEALER
items.summary: Find out how a new obfuscated loader evades static detection using .reloc section abuse, five anti-VM/language checks and MBA obfuscation to deliver infostealer malware via Google Ads.

items.title: How the Peter Thiel-Linked Dialog Club Secretly Ranks Its Members
items.summary: Leaked files show the invite-only network grades members by their money and fame, shaping who’s in, who’s out, and who pays.

items.title: Novo Nordisk Breach Exposes Software Development Pipeline Risk
items.summary: A leaked GitHub token underscores what most organizations get wrong: Treating secrets management as a tooling problem rather than an identity problem.

items.title: Operation Escaneo Signals Shift in LatAm Threat Landscape
items.summary: The threat group's curious business model may combine opportunistic monetization alongside intel collection, without much coordination between the two.

items.title: FIFA Bug Exposes World Cup Streams to Remote Takeover
items.summary: A hacker could have "Rickrolled" the World Cup — or worse — thanks to FIFA's unenforced Entra access controls.

items.title: Close Encounters of the Human Kind
items.summary: In the latest Threat Source, Hazel channels her inner Spielberg to explore why humans are delightfully irrational, reminding us that while security best practices are simple in theory, they’re a lot harder to pull off when you’re busy dealing with real life.

items.title: ‘Popa’ Botnet Linked to Publicly-Traded Israeli Firm
items.summary: For the past four years, a sprawling Android-based botnet called Popa has forced millions of consumer TV boxes to relay Internet traffic linked to advertising fraud, account takeovers, and mass data-scraping efforts. items.title: Salesforce Data Thefts Continue via Klue App Compromise
items.summary: Klue's Battlecards is now the third integrated application that has been compromised to steal customers' Salesforce data, and victims include Huntress, the cybersecurity vendor.

items.title: Fake GitHub Stars and AI Videos Mask a Crypto Clipper
items.summary: A Rust crypto clipper hides behind fake GitHub stars and AI-narrated YouTube videos
